text: A search system should not only match similar words. If the user asks for the conclusion, the system should rank posts with matching conclusions higher.
The site is slow because every page loads too many unrelated assets. Reducing unnecessary assets should improve response time.
A definition is useful when terms are ambiguous. In this experiment, structure reranking means changing the order of baseline search results using extracted reasoning structures.
Vector search can find topical similarity, but it may confuse formatting similarity with argument similarity. Structure extraction can separate those signals.
This approach is not a replacement for dense retrieval. It is a small reranking layer that tests whether conclusions, premises, causes, contrasts, and definitions improve search.
Markdown lists often look similar even when the ideas are unrelated. Formatting should not dominate the search score when the query asks about reasoning.
If a post claims that public experiments improve trust, and another post argues the opposite from the same premise, both should be discoverable for comparison.
A causal explanation connects a reason to an outcome. For example, if extraction errors are frequent, reranking can become worse.
This keyword trap repeats search, conclusions, posts, rank, and higher. It has similar words but does not state that conclusions should affect ranking.
A contrast compares two ideas. Topical similarity is about shared subject matter, while argument similarity is about shared reasoning path.
Reranking is not a replacement for dense retrieval. It should run after candidate generation and only adjust the order of candidates.
If structure extraction invents a premise, reranking becomes worse because the score rewards a false explanation.
A premise is a reason that supports a conclusion. A conclusion is the claim that the reasoning tries to justify.
When two posts share the same premise but reach opposite conclusions, the search interface should show them side by side.
Causal causal causal, reranking reranking, worse worse. This post is a keyword trap and does not explain any cause.
Structure search means storing extracted conclusions, premises, causes, contrasts, and definitions as explicit searchable fields.
Markdown lists are useful for scanning, but they should not count as reasoning evidence by themselves.
Public experiments can improve trust because readers can inspect the hypothesis, method, failures, and results.
Public experiments can reduce trust if the report hides failures or presents a tiny synthetic sample as proof.
A definition answers what a term means. A causal explanation answers why an outcome happened.
